# Single sign-on (SSO)

## About markdown versions

All pages in this area are available in clean markdown format. To access the markdown version of any page, append `/index.md` to the page URL.

Example:
- HTML: `https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/`
- Markdown: `https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/index.md`


## Platform Administration


- [About Early Access](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/about/): This document guides you through the process of joining and getting the most out of the Early Access program.

- [About Tealium Prism](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/tealium-prism/about-tealium-prism/): Tealium Prism is a native mobile SDK for iOS (Swift) and Android (Kotlin) built on a unified, reactive event pipeline.

- [Databricks Delta Sharing cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/data-sources/databricks-delta-sharing-cloud-data-source/): This article describes how to set up the Databricks Delta Sharing cloud data source.

- [Navigation menu](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/resources/navigation-menu/): We introduced a refreshed and unified navigation menu, designed to streamline user workflows, improve accessibility, and support future product expansion.

- [PII Permissions and Server-Side Account Level Roles (Early Access)](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/administration/platform-permissions/pii-account-roles/): PII Permissions with server-side account level roles is an Early Access feature that introduces data privacy controls for server-side products.

- [Quick start guide for Tealium Prism](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/tealium-prism/quick-start/): Set up Tealium Prism for iOS and Android by installing the SDK, initializing a configuration, and sending your first events with data layer values.

- [Trino cloud data source](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/data-sources/trino-cloud-data-source/): This article describes how to set up the Trino cloud data source.

- [Visitor Deletion Jobs (Early Access)](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/data-storage/visitor-deletion-jobs/): This article describes how to set up automated visitor deletion jobs to delete visitor records from the Tealium Customer Data Hub.

- [Webhook OAuth2 2-legged mTLS connector](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/server-side-connectors/webhook-oauth2-2-legged-mtls/): Configure the webhook connector to authenticate with your endpoint using a mutual TLS client certificate generated by Tealium.

- [Account organization](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/account-organization/): This document explains how accounts are organized into profiles, versions, and permissions. Use this hierarchy to help organize your Tealium account to match your business needs and implement a smooth development workflow.

- [Set up Single Sign-On for Tealium Accounts](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/single-sign-on/sso/): This article describes how to set up Single Sign-On (SSO) for an account.

- [About audiences](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/audiences/about-audiences-early-access/): This article provides information about audiences, segments, and visitor retention time.

- [About log streaming](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/log-streaming/about/): This article explains how to use log streaming to route Tealium system logs to external observability, storage, and alerting platforms.

- [About platform permissions](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/about-platform-permissions/): This article describes the unified platform permissions system.

- [About SCIM API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/about/): Learn how to use SCIM provisioning to sync users from your identity provider to your Tealium account.

- [Audience dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/audiences/audience-dashboard/): This article provides information about the audience dashboard.

- [Single Sign-On (SSO) Certificate Status](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/single-sign-on/certificates/): This article describes how to view the status of a SAML 2.0 signing certificate.

- [Manage Single Sign-On (SSO) Connections](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/single-sign-on/manage/): This article describes how to manage SSO connections.

- [Permissions system migration guide](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/permissions-system-migration-guide/migration-about/): This document explains Tealium's migration plan for platform permissions and the benefits of migration.

- [SSO configuration with ADFS (Active Directory) IdP](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/single-sign-on/adfs/): This article describes how to configure ADFS (Active Directory) to access and download a metadata file for creating a new Tealium SSO connection.

- [Admin roles](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/admin-roles/): This article describes admin roles in the platform permissions system.

- [Manage audiences](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/audiences/manage-audiences-early-access/): This article provides information about creating, editing, and deleting audiences, as well as activating audiences and creating segments.

- [Proactive notification contacts](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/administration/proactive-notification-contacts/): This article explains proactive notification contacts and how to specify them for time-sensitive service communications.

- [SCIM and identity provider configuration](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/example/): Learn how to configure SCIM with a supported identity provider.

- [SSO configuration with Azure IdP](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/single-sign-on/azure/): This article describes how to configure Azure to access and download a metadata file for creating a new Tealium SSO connection.

- [Manage enrichments](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/attributes-improvements/enrichments/): This article describes enrichments and how to add them.

- [SSO configuration with Okta IdP](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/single-sign-on/okta/): This article describes how to configure Okta to access and download a metadata file for creating a new Tealium SSO connection.

- [Permission groups](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/permission-groups/): This article describes how to manage permission groups in the platform permissions system.

- [POST Group API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/post-group-api/): The POST Group API creates a new group in the account.

- [POST User API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/post-user-api/): The POST User API creates a new user in the account.

- [General Strategy](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/permissions-system-migration-guide/strategy/): This document describes a general strategy to follow when building a migration plan for the permissions system.

- [Stitching merge rules](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/visitor-stitching/stitching-merge-rules/): Stitching merge rules are optional, per‑attribute rules that determine the final value of an attribute when two visitor profiles are stitched.

- [GET Group API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/get-group-api/): The GET Group API retrieves an existing group from the account.

- [GET User API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/get-user-api/): The GET User API retrieves an existing user from the account.

- [Multi-factor authentication (MFA)](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/multi-factor-authentication-mfa/): Multi-Factor Authentication is a security method which requires a second method of authentication to log in, such as a smartphone or browser app.

- [Users](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/users/): This article describes how to manage users on your account in the platform permissions system.

- [Multi-factor authentication FAQ](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/mfa-faq/): This article contains frequently asked questions and troubleshooting tips for Multi-Factor Authentication on your Tealium account.

- [Password policy](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/password-policy/): A password policy is a set of rules designed to improve security by establishing strict password requirements for your users. These rules are enforced on all users in your account.

- [Session timeout](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/session-timeout/): This article describes how to configure the session expiration time for your Tealium iQ Tag Management account.

- [Tealium IP addresses to allow](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/ip-allow-list/): This article provides a list of IP addresses to include in your allowlist to enable the Tealium platform to communicate with external systems.

- [GET Groups API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/get-groups-api/): The GET Groups API retrieves a list of groups from the account.

- [GET Users API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/get-users-api/): The GET Users API retrieves a list of users from the account.

- [Moments API and CloudStream integration guide](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/moments-api/moments-api-cloudstream-integration/): Configure Moments API to retrieve real-time visitor data from CloudStream segments.

- [Manage and generate API keys](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/security-access/api-keys/): This article describes how to manage and generate API keys used to authenticate with the Tealium API.

- [Manage log streaming](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/log-streaming/manage-log-streaming/): This article explains how to configure, verify, and manage log streaming destinations and log sources.

- [Plan](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/permissions-system-migration-guide/plan/): This document describes how to plan your migration to the platform permissions system.

- [PATCH Group API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/patch-group-api/): The PATCH Group API updates an existing group in the account.

- [PATCH User API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/patch-user-api/): The PATCH User API updates an existing user in the account.

- [PUT Group API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/put-group-api/): The PUT Group API replaces an existing group in the account.

- [PUT User API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/put-user-api/): The PUT User API replaces an existing user in the account.

- [DELETE Group API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/delete-group-api/): The DELETE Group API removes an existing group from the account.

- [DELETE User API](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/api/scim-api/delete-user-api/): The DELETE User API removes an existing user from the account.

- [Tealium feedback policy](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/resources/feedback-policy/): This document explains our feedback policy.

- [Compatible browsers](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/resources/compatible-browsers/): This article describes the browsers that the Tealium platform supports.

- [Profile retention policy](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/resources/profile-retention-policy/): This document explains our account profile retention policy.

- [Connector error logging](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/early-access/log-streaming/connector-error-logging/): This article explains how to configure connector error log sources, review log parameters, and troubleshoot connector errors.

- [Tealium AI Features](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/resources/ai-features/): Tealium offers AI features to enhance your experience in the product interface.

- [Deployment Strategies](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/permissions-system-migration-guide/deployment-strategies/): This document describes example deployment strategies for migrating to the platform permissions system.

- [Permissions Enforcement](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/account-org/permissions-system-migration-guide/permissions-enforcement/): This document describes how to use Permissions Enforcement in the platform permissions system.

- [Tealium status page](https://docs.tealium.com/administration/resources/tealium-status-page/): This document explains the status page and how to subscribe to announcements.
